div.head:
EXHIBIT V.
Annex 6.

p.hi: Bark Cape Horn Pigeon—Owners’ outward account, 1892, inward account, and interest account to January 20, 1892, with J. and W. R. Wing.

row.cell: New casks to C. W. Morgan.
By balance to debit owners’ account due this date
To transfer oil casks at San Francisco, from last outward account, on hand
To paid labor packing boat boards, $1.20; boat boards, $13.50
Storage goods (marine) at California to January 3, 1891
Paid Cornell & Co., coopers, at California, labor making over 10,975 gallons oil shooks, and shooking 1 gang shooks (stored free expense)
J. and W. R. Wing & Co., clothing, etc
Ship Com. T. Hallen, freight goods
To storage goods at California to Jan. 10, 1892.
To paid telegrams at times account business
To paid sundry bills at San Francisco:
McNab & Smith, carting goods to store
Whittier, Fuller & Co., paint and oil
Cornell & Co., storage and freight paid
Use team, $18.45; labor, B. F. Wing, $60.77
Share expenses, Wm. R. Wing, to and from California
Labor, $8.05; E. Pierce, gun and lances, $50
A. B. Copper Co., metal used at California
Paid share ticket and expenses Captain Scullun, home and return
J. W. Gawin and Co., hose, etc
Telegram to master, $1.50; cooking in stream, $7
Dillon & Co., marine glass and rep. glasses
L. Foard, mast hoops and blocks
O. H. Keys, meats and vegetables in stream
Paid Simpson & Fisher, mates’ boat sail
Clabrough, Golcher & Co., repairs to gun.
Labor, looking after goods, free, N. B
Paid advances to officers and crew
To paid balance interest account to date
To paid 2½ per cent commissions on outfits, say, $10,187.50

div.closer:
(E. & O. E.)
New Bedford, January 20, 1892.
Case: Cape Horn Pigeon.
Deposition of William R. Wing.
Exhibit 7.
(Initialed) W. F. C.
